texts.full_text: ystolic pulmonary artery pressure (sPAP), and disease-specific biomarkers. One hundred twenty patients (60.9 ± 8.8 years; 75.0% men; LVESV: median [interquartile range] =53 mL) were enrolled with a follow-up of >90%. There was no difference in change from baseline to crossover between groups for the primary end point (-19 ml, -11/-32 vs-17ml,-12/33 P=0.81). Changes favoring BiV-CRT included LVEF (7% [2%-13] versus 1 [-5%/6], p = .02), RV dimensions and function at baseline but not crossover period (+10 mm[4 to14]/+3, -8/-18 vs +9mm [+0.08/15/+11ml; P=ns). LVESV decreased in both groups (BiVR-CRT: 25 ml [-39%]; LVC-RCT:-17 (-33%) at baseline and-23(-43)and –14(–32), respectively, p = ns]. Both pacing strategies resultedin a decrease of MR by ≥1 grade during follow-up. BiV vs LV CRT had similar effects on changes in disease-specific biomarkers (natriuretic peptide A: 11 pg/mL [-15/38] versus -3 (-9)/26; P=ns) and systolic pulmonary artery pressure (+6 [0-12]/+4 mm Hg, p = ns). Both pacing strategies had similar effects on structural cardiac remodelling in patients with moderate to severe heart failure. A significant reduction of MR was seen irrespective the numberof leads implanted. Impacto de la estimulación biventricular frente a monoventrículo en pacientes con insuficiencia cardiaca: análisis del ensayo Evaluation Resynchronization Therapy for Heart Failure (EARTH).
